scope_boundary: Lean finite-record proof wiring plus one hand-authored semantic-depth classification; no arbitrary abstraction correctness, deployed implementation binding, runtime enforcement, historical-project behavior, or broad system guarantee is promoted
transition_reason: The current 225 proof targets are wired through the generated manifest and audits, the Lean workspace builds 66 jobs, and one hand-authored record distinguishes field presence, finite route, derived invariant, executable-model bridge, and absent implementation binding while rejecting ten laundering mutations. The semantic-adequacy audit is an internal classification, and the new fixture does not establish that arbitrary abstraction maps are correct or that any deployed service enforces the modeled rule.
required_artifacts: tracked executable-model bridges for any finite predicate proposed to support behavior
artifact-bound and independently observed implementation effects before runtime language is allowed
accepted evidence transition for any narrower statement proposed above argument
verification_command: (cd lean && lake build) && python3 scripts/validate_proof_artifact_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_proof_adequacy_review.py && python3 scripts/validate_formal_semantic_depth.py
negative_results: semantic adequacy remains an internal classification rather than proof of abstraction correctness
finite-record proofs do not prove broad runtime behavior
implementation binding is absent in the depth fixture
proof build does not promote source interpretation
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed statement whose abstraction map, assumptions, executable bridge, implementation binding, observed effect, limitations, and accepted evidence transition match the exact consumer claim.
limitations: Lean proves only the imported finite predicates
schemas validate shape and tests validate behavior in separate lanes
no runtime property follows from these proofs alone
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent formal-methods audit
acceptance_blockers: no proof that the hand-authored abstraction map is generally correct
no deployed implementation binding or observed enforcement effect
claim text remains broader than the bounded proof and depth fixtures
non_claims: does not claim broad proof of safety
does not claim the Lean targets are semantically adequate
does not promote any chapter claim
